title: taraxacum circinatum
intro: Taraxacum circinatum, also known as the crested dandelion or hooked dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Taraxacum circinatum include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum circinatum can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support liver and kidney health, as well as to stimulate digestion.
